verse_text: For he is not a Jew who is one outwardly, nor is circumcision that which is outward in the flesh;

links.context: The Smyrnan church was poor, perhaps because of the persecution, but spiritually rich, the opposite of Laodicea. The Jews of the synagogue of Satan may be either Jews in name only (see [[ROM.2:28]], [[ROM.2:29]]) who were insincere in their profession of Judaism and compromising in their beliefs ([[JOH.8:31]]-47); or Jews who refused to embrace the Messiah and His Church ([[ROM.2:28]]; [[GAL.6:15]]).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The Smyrnan church was poor, perhaps because of the persecution, but spiritually rich, the opposite of Laodicea. The Jews of the synagogue of Satan may be either Jews in name only (see [[ROM.2:28]], [[ROM.2:29]]) who were insincere in their profession of Judaism and compromising in their beliefs ([[JOH.8:31]]-47); or Jews who refused to embrace the Messiah and His Church ([[ROM.2:28]]; [[GAL.6:15]]).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The Lord commanded a census to be taken in Israel to draft an army for war. This census shows the militant nature of Israel in warfare. Israel was a type of the Church, "the Israel of God" ([[GAL.6:16]]; see [[ROM.2:28]], [[ROM.2:29]]), and the Church is also militant. However, the warfare of the Church is spiritual and evangelistic in nature ([[EPH.6:10]]-20). The spiritual enemies are the sinful passions, death, and the devil. Evangelism is to bring as many as will come into the kingdom of God and is to continue up to the end, when the kingdom will be fully established ([[MAT.24:14]]).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: The tribes of Jacob include all those of the true Israel, in both the Old and New Covenants (see [[ROM.2:26]]-29).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
